label: Trello
description: Connect to Trello to read and manage kanban boards, lists, and cards from your Atlassian workspace.

form.title: Connect Trello
form.subtitle: Paste your Trello API key and token to give Anton access to your boards.

methods.label: API Key + Token
methods.description: Paste your Trello API key and a personal token — both generated at trello.com/app-key in seconds. Simplest path with no OAuth setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Get your Trello API Key and Token

### Steps

1. Open [https://trello.com/app-key](https://trello.com/app-key) while logged in to Trello.
2. You'll see your **API Key** near the top of the page. Copy it and paste it into the **API Key** field below.
3. On the same page, click the **Token** hyperlink (labeled something like "you can manually generate a token").
4. Trello will ask you to authorize the app — click **Allow**.
5. The next page shows a long string — that is your **Token**. Copy it and paste it into the **Token** field below.

### Notes

- The token is shown once on screen but can be regenerated at any time from the same page.
- Tokens can be revoked at [https://trello.com/your-username/account](https://trello.com) under **Settings → Applications**.
- If you are a Trello Power-Up developer or need organization-level access, make sure the token has read/write permission when prompted during the authorize step.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Your Trello API key, found at trello.com/app-key.

fields.name: token
fields.label: Token
fields.placeholder: 64-character token from the Token link on trello.com/app-key
fields.description: Personal token generated by clicking the Token link on the same page as your API key.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Bring Your Own Client)
methods.description: Use your own Atlassian/Trello OAuth 2.0 client credentials for a browser-based authorization flow. Requires creating a Power-Up in the Trello developer portal.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 via Trello Power-Up

### Steps

1. Go to [https://trello.com/power-ups/admin](https://trello.com/power-ups/admin) and click **New**.
2. Fill in a name (e.g. Anton) and associate it with a workspace.
3. Once created, open your Power-Up and go to the **API Key** tab.
4. Note the **OAuth Client ID** and generate an **OAuth Secret**.
5. Add `http://localhost` as an allowed redirect URL under OAuth settings.
6. Paste the Client ID and Client Secret into the fields below and click **Submit**.

### Notes

- This path is more complex than the API Key + Token method — use that instead unless you specifically need OAuth delegation.
- The Trello OAuth flow uses OAuth 1.0a under the hood; scopes map to read/write access.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: Found on the API Key tab of your Power-Up at trello.com/power-ups/admin.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Generated alongside the client ID in your Power-Up settings.
